Name: Art Opportunities Monthly
What it is: Listing platform where organizations POST open calls (grants, juried shows, public art); links out to your own submission URL.
How to submit a call: Use the public 'Create Post' web form (no account) — enter call title, deadline, description and your external submission link; it publishes to the feed.
Audience/reach: Thousands of working artists; site features over 6,900 organizations and 18,000+ total opportunities (Art Opportunities).
Notes/gotchas: Free to post your own call and link to your external submission page. No requirement to use their submission system.

Name: Art3000 (Call for Artists)
What it is: Free art portal where anyone can submit a Call for Artists / Call for Entries with no registration required.
How to submit a call: Go to the 'Submit Call for Artists / Call for Entries' page → fill the public web form (title, deadline, description, your external submission URL) → submit. No registration needed; appears on the Call for Artists archive.
Audience/reach: Artists browsing Art3000's Call for Artists archive
Notes/gotchas: Confirmed free by NOT REAL ART team (Kirsten). Submission is free and requires no account; links to your own submission page. Optional free art gallery for displaying works (needs name/alias + email).

Name: ArtCallEntry
What it is: Free searchable database + newsletter where organizations advertise their calls for entry, linking out to their own site.
Cost (to organizer): Free (featured placement / email ads paid — contact for rates)
How to submit a call: Go to 'Submit a Call (Free)' → fill the 'Add a New Opportunity' form (title, your external website URL, description, contact) → submit → click the confirmation link in the verification email to publish.
Audience/reach: Artists browsing ArtCallEntry's database + newsletter subscribers
Notes/gotchas: Completely free for art organizations to advertise a call for entry online; links to your external submission URL. Sign up for their newsletter audience too. (Featured placement / email ads are paid — contact for rates.)

Name: ArtDeadline.Com
What it is: Major opportunity directory where arts organizations POST their own open calls; artists click through to your submission URL.
How to submit a call: Submit your call via their listing form (free if artist entry fee ≤ $50). Enter details + your external submission URL; staff posts it to the ADC news feed.
Audience/reach: Thousands of artists and art institutions worldwide (ArtDeadline.Com)
Notes/gotchas: Free if your call's artist entry fee is $50 or less and not internet-only; otherwise $10. You link out to your own submission page (notrealart.com / artsvilleusa.com). High-impact ad upgrades from $40.

Name: College Art Association (CAA)
What it is: A professional association for the visual arts that provides a platform to post calls for entries, grants, and exhibition opportunities, as well as a separate job board.
How to submit a call: Members submit via the 'Submit an Opportunity' form — fill in the call details and link to your own application page; CAA publishes it to the Opportunities board.
Audience/reach: Thousands of artists, scholars, museum professionals, and educators in the visual arts community (CAA)
Notes/gotchas: Submitting 'Opportunities' (calls for entries, grants, residencies, etc.) is free via a web form. Employment job listings are separate and require a fee starting at $590 for non-members via the Career Center.

Name: GivingTuesday
What it is: A global generosity movement and annual day of giving (the Tuesday after Thanksgiving).
Audience/reach: Millions of donors globally; $3.1 billion raised in the US in 2022 (GivingTuesday)
Notes/gotchas: There is no central registration or master directory for nonprofits; organizations are encouraged to run their own independent campaigns using the provided toolkit and resources. (GivingTuesday FAQ)

Name: InLiquid
What it is: A nonprofit organization that provides resources and exhibition opportunities for artists and the arts community in Philadelphia.
How to submit a call: Complete InLiquid's Airtable intake form with your open-call details and external submission URL; the team reviews and posts it.
Audience/reach: Approx. 120+ artists per event and 2,000+ residents at certain locations; reaches the Philadelphia arts community (InLiquid).
Notes/gotchas: Submissions are for posting opportunities like exhibitions, grants, and residencies on InLiquid's platform. The submission is done via an Airtable form and likely undergoes manual review before being posted. (InLiquid Opportunities)

Name: OpenCall.ca
What it is: Curated, continuously-updated index of art grants, open calls, exhibitions and residencies worldwide; community-submitted.
How to submit a call: Click '+ SUBMIT' in the header → fill the 'Submit an Opportunity' form (Title, Organisation, Type, Deadline, Official URL = your submission page) → 'Submit for Review' → appears live after curation.
Audience/reach: Artists browsing OpenCall.ca's curated global opportunity index
Notes/gotchas: Canadian-based but features international opportunities. No account needed; listing reviewed before going live and links to your official URL. Has a Donate button but no posting fee.

Name: Mid-America Arts Alliance (M-AAA)
What it is: A regional arts organization that provides grants, programs, and exhibitions to support the arts in a six-state region.
Audience/reach: Artists and arts organizations across a six-state US region (AR, KS, MO, NE, OK, TX); over $2 million in funding provided since 2012 (Mid-America Arts Alliance).
Geography: US region (AR, KS, MO, NE, OK, TX)
Notes/gotchas: Grant applications require 501(c)(3) status, a Unique Entity Identifier (UEI) from SAM.gov, and often a dollar-for-dollar match (Mid-America Arts Alliance). Regional Touring Program applications are rolling within the fiscal year.

Name: All About Photo
What it is: Photography portal where organizations POST photo contests/open calls; links out to your own submission URL (photo-focused).
How to submit a call: Register, add a reciprocal backlink to All About Photo on your site, then submit your photo call via your account; links out to your submission page.
Audience/reach: Thousands of photography lovers and experts (estimate from site)
Notes/gotchas: Free if you add a reciprocal backlink. Photo-focused audience. Links to your own submission page.

Name: ArtCall.org
What it is: Lets organizations POST a free promotional open-call listing that links out to your own submission website.
How to submit a call: Create a free account → 'Add Your Call' → choose the free promotional (advertising) listing → enter details and your external submission URL (skip their paid jurying software).
Audience/reach: 250,000+ registered artists (ArtCall Overview)
Notes/gotchas: Free promotional (advertising) listing links out to your own submission site (physical-display calls). ArtCall.org also sells paid jurying software (you don't need it) and has partnered with ArtCallEntry.com where you can ALSO list for free. Account required to post.

Name: ArtRabbit
What it is: International art platform where organizations POST open calls; listings link directly to your own submission URL.
Cost (to organizer): Free (fee-free calls, ~5/week) / £100 standard / free w/ Premium £99/yr
How to submit a call: Submit your call through ArtRabbit's Google Form. Fee-free calls can get a free listing (~5/week); listing links out to your opportunity URL after review.
Audience/reach: 70,000+ professional users and students; 23,500 weekly newsletter subscribers (ArtRabbit) (ArtRabbit)
Notes/gotchas: Verified org-perspective: ArtRabbit subsidizes free listings for calls with NO artist entry fee (about 5/week). Listings link out to YOUR opportunity URL — no submission lock-in. Curated via a Google Form. UK/Europe-leaning audience.

Name: Artsy
What it is: Artsy's editorial pitch and press contact page for partners and contributors to submit story ideas and news.
Audience/reach: 3.4 million+ monthly collectors and enthusiasts (Source: Artsy About Page)
Notes/gotchas: Pitches should be sent to pitches@artsy.net. The site emphasizes original, timely narratives with high-quality images. Editorial coverage is a mix of in-house reporting and freelancer/partner pitches.

Name: CallFor.org
What it is: Long-running art open-calls platform (awards, exhibitions, residencies, competitions) with paid listings and a free tier for unfunded artist-run initiatives.
Cost (to organizer): From 45€ standard (60€ / 75€ upgrades); FREE for artist-run/unfunded orgs via refundable 20€ deposit
How to submit a call: General Menu → Standard Upload → choose European or Non-European → fill the form (header, content, deadline, your website URL) → submit → pay via card/PayPal (or deposit for free tier) → staff reviews and posts within 1–2 days.
Audience/reach: International artist audience browsing CallFor.org
Notes/gotchas: Based in NL; non-EU posts exclude 21% VAT. Upload form has an 'Open call Website address' field so artists go to YOUR page. The free tier requires you to be artist-run with NO funding/sponsors (20€ deposit refunded minus small transaction fee). Reviewed & posted in 1–2 working days.

Name: DAFgiving360 (formerly Schwab Charitable)
What it is: The nonprofit resources and information page for one of the largest US donor-advised fund (DAF) sponsors (formerly Schwab Charitable), providing tools like DAF Direct and fundraising guidance for organizations to engage with DAF donors.
Audience/reach: 165,000+ charities supported in 2025; $9.9 billion granted in 2025 (DAFgiving360)
Notes/gotchas: Schwab Charitable has rebranded to DAFgiving360. There is no dedicated self-service portal for nonprofits; organizations are primarily identified via IRS records and Guidestar/Candid profiles. Nonprofits can implement the DAF Direct widget to facilitate grants from donors (DAFgiving360). For information updates or EFT inquiries, nonprofits must contact Donor Services directly (DAFgiving360 Privacy). Also add the free DAF Direct donation widget (dafdirect.org) to your site so DAF donors can give directly.

Name: EntryThingy (free call listing)
What it is: Submission-management platform that ALSO offers a free path to list a call created elsewhere, linking to your own submission page.
How to submit a call: Open the 'Add a Call for Entry' form → enter title, organization, email, description, deadline, start date and the 'Link to your Call for Entry Page' (your external URL) → submit for review → publishes to their call lists.
Audience/reach: Artists browsing EntryThingy's aggregated call lists
Notes/gotchas: Use ONLY the 'add a call already created elsewhere' form — it links to YOUR submission page and is free. Do NOT buy their paid jurying system since you have your own platform.

Name: Fractured Atlas Artist Opportunity Database
What it is: A searchable database of artist grants, funders, awards, and open calls curated by Fractured Atlas.
How to submit a call: Submit your opportunity via their intake form; staff manually reviews and adds it to the public Notion opportunity database, linking to your page.
Audience/reach: Largest association of artists in the U.S. (reaches tens of thousands of US-based artists across all disciplines)
Notes/gotchas: Submissions are reviewed before being added to the database; recommends submitting at least 8 weeks before the deadline.

Name: Givebutter
What it is: All-in-one fundraising platform for nonprofits to create campaigns, collect donations, and manage donors.
Cost (to organizer): Free (0% platform fee with donor tips enabled; 3% platform fee if tips disabled) plus standard processing fees (2.9% + $0.30) (Givebutter Pricing)
Audience/reach: 10,000,000+ changemakers (donors and organizers) (Givebutter)
Best for: donor, open call, exhibition, grant, press
Notes/gotchas: Standard processing fees apply; platform fee is $0 only if donor tips are enabled. Advanced features available via 'Plus' paid plans starting at $29/mo (Givebutter Pricing).

Name: GoFundMe
What it is: A global crowdfunding platform that allows individuals and nonprofits to raise money for various causes.
Cost (to organizer): Free to start; 2.2% + $0.30 transaction fee per donation for verified charities (GoFundMe Pricing)
Audience/reach: Over 200 million people (GoFundMe)
Notes/gotchas: Nonprofits must be verified 501(c)(3) organizations to receive the reduced transaction fee rate; higher volume organizations may use GoFundMe Pro (Classy) (Zeffy)

Name: Juxtapoz Art & Culture Magazine
What it is: Leading art and culture magazine for contemporary, lowbrow, and street art.
Audience/reach: 1.3M+ followers on Instagram (Source), ~65K-250K monthly readers/visits (Similarweb, Media Kit); reaching global art enthusiasts and professionals.
Notes/gotchas: Submission is via a Jotform on the contact page; select 'Submit Your Work' from the dropdown. Direct email for the CTO is also provided. Magazine lead times are long.

Name: OpenCall Radar
What it is: Curated aggregator of artist open calls, residencies, grants and prizes that directs artists to external application pages.
Cost (to organizer): confirm (no self-service posting; email to suggest a source)
How to submit a call: No web form. Email hello@opencallradar.com to suggest your open call as a source; their team curates whether to include it (links to your external page).
Audience/reach: Artists browsing OpenCall Radar's curated feed (sourced from 30+ sites)
Notes/gotchas: No self-service posting portal — it curates from 30+ sources and links artists to external URLs. To get listed, email hello@opencallradar.com to suggest your call as a source. Lower-priority / passive channel.

Name: The Art Guide
What it is: Free online directory of exhibitions, galleries and calls for artists where organizations post their own listings.
How to submit a call: Create a free 'Gallery Account' → sign in → in the dashboard click 'Post a new listing' → choose Call for Entries → fill in details and your external submission URL → publishes.
Audience/reach: Artists browsing The Art Guide's calls-for-artists directory
Notes/gotchas: Galleries, museums, art associations and nonprofits may post Exhibition Listings, Calls for Entries and Events at NO charge; listing links to your own submission page. No charge to create an account or post. (Paid featured/ad upgrades optional.)

Name: The Art List
What it is: Database where organizations POST contests/juried shows/exhibitions; artists link through to your own submission URL.
How to submit a call: Register for a free account → add your call in the dashboard (Basic = free) → enter details + your external submission link. Paid upgrades for featured placement.
Audience/reach: 50,000+ members, 25,000+ email subscribers, and 87,000+ social media followers (artists and photographers worldwide)
Notes/gotchas: Free basic listing links to your own submission page. Paid upgrades ($50+) for featured placement. No submission lock-in.

Name: Artist Communities Alliance (ACA)
What it is: An international association and directory platform for artist residency programs and arts organizations to list open calls, jobs, and residency opportunities.
How to submit a call: Join/sign in as an organizational member, then post your open call / residency opportunity to the ACA opportunity board linking to your own page.
Audience/reach: 400+ member organizations and programs reaching artists globally (Artist Communities Alliance)
Notes/gotchas: Membership is free as of January 2025 but required to list in the directory; requires commitment to the network's shared values and affirmations (Artist Communities Alliance).

Name: Benevity
What it is: A global corporate purpose platform that connects nonprofits with corporate giving, matching, and volunteering programs.
Cost (to organizer): Free to register; 2.9% average platform fee plus merchant fees (<2.5%) (Benevity FAQs, Fee Schedule)
Audience/reach: 2 million+ organizations worldwide with over $10 billion in donations processed (Benevity, Press Release)
Notes/gotchas: Registration requires vetting and documentation (e.g., IRS Determination Letter). Manual check fees apply in North America (greater of $25/7% up to $100); electronic funds transfer is strongly recommended to avoid these (Benevity FAQ).

Name: California Arts Council
What it is: State arts agency providing grant funding to California-based arts organizations and individual artists (California Arts Council).
Audience/reach: California arts community (approx. 39M residents, reaching artists and nonprofits statewide) (California Arts Council)
Notes/gotchas: Requires 501(c)(3) status or fiscal sponsor, a principal place of business in California, and a minimum two-year history of arts programming (California Arts Council FAQ).

Name: Connectively (formerly HARO)
What it is: A platform that connects subject matter experts (sources) with journalists and publishers seeking quotes and insights for their articles.
Cost (to organizer): Free ($0 for 3 answers/mo) | Lite $11/mo (billed yearly) | Pro $29/mo (billed yearly) | Business $59/mo (billed yearly) | $6.99 per extra credit
Audience/reach: Reach to millions via 1,000+ top publishers (e.g., Fast Company, Fortune, CNET) with 50,000+ experts enrolled.
Notes/gotchas: Formerly known as Help A Reporter Out (HARO). The free tier is limited to 3 answers per month; additional submissions require a paid subscription or individual credits. Requires setting up a profile and monitoring keyword alerts.

Name: Creative Capital
What it is: National nonprofit supporting individual artists through grants, professional development, and a curated list of artist opportunities.
Cost (to organizer): Free (sponsored listing included with $5,000/yr membership, or via manual email contact)
Audience/reach: 250,000+ artists (per site)
Notes/gotchas: Submissions for 'Artist Opportunities' are community-driven but require manual email contact or are provided as a benefit of the $5,000/yr Institutional Membership. There is no public-facing instant submission web form.

Name: Donorbox
What it is: An online fundraising platform that allows nonprofits to create customizable donation forms, crowdfunding campaigns, and peer-to-peer pages (Donorbox).
Audience/reach: 100,000+ nonprofits worldwide; reaches a global donor base (Donorbox Pricing)
Notes/gotchas: Standard plan has no monthly fee but charges platform fees (2.95% - 3.95%); donors can often opt to cover these fees (Donorbox Pricing).

Name: Every.org
What it is: A nonprofit-run fundraising platform that provides free tools for nonprofits to accept donations (cash, stocks, DAFs, crypto) and a discovery network for donors.
Cost (to organizer): Free (0% platform fees; processing fees apply for card/crypto) (Every.org)
Audience/reach: 8,500+ nonprofits and their donor base (Every.org)
Notes/gotchas: Requires 501(c)(3) status or fiscal sponsor; admin access approval typically takes ~2 business days (Every.org Support).

Name: Fidelity Charitable
What it is: The largest donor-advised fund (DAF) sponsor in the US, allowing donors to manage charitable accounts and recommend grants to nonprofits.
Audience/reach: Nearly 400,000 active donors who recommended $18.3 billion in grants to ~227,000 charities in 2025 (Fidelity Charitable).
Notes/gotchas: IRS-qualified 501(c)(3) public charities are typically listed automatically based on IRS data; however, electronic enrollment (EFT) is required to receive grant disbursements and will be mandatory starting July 2026 (GiveChariot).

Name: Foundation Directory (Candid)
What it is: The premier grant prospect research database for identifying foundation and corporate funders.
Cost (to organizer): Free tier available; Premium starts at $1,199/yr for nonprofits; Free for nonprofits <$1M revenue with Gold Seal (Candid) (Spark the Fire)
Audience/reach: Industry-standard database of 240,000+ grantmakers for nonprofit fundraisers (Candid)
Notes/gotchas: Merged with GuideStar into "Candid search" in Jan 2026; free access requires Gold Seal of Transparency; significant price hike for larger orgs (GlobeNewswire) (LinkedIn)

Name: GrantWatch
What it is: A leading grant listing directory and search engine for nonprofits, businesses, and individuals to find funding opportunities GrantWatch.
Audience/reach: Approx. 2.23M grant recipient profiles and 374k funder profiles; reaches a wide audience of nonprofit and small business subscribers GrantWatch.
Notes/gotchas: Submissions are subject to manual verification and approval by the GrantWatch editorial team before being listed GrantWatch. Primarily a database for grant seekers to find funding.

Name: Instrumentl
What it is: An institutional fundraising platform that combines grant prospecting, tracking, and management into one tool for nonprofits (Instrumentl).
Cost (to organizer): Free (14-day trial), then starts at $299/mo (billed annually) or $349/mo (monthly) (Instrumentl)
Audience/reach: 15,000+ grant professionals across 5,500+ nonprofits and consultancy firms (Instrumentl)
Notes/gotchas: Requires paid membership after 14-day trial; trial does not require a credit card (Instrumentl).

Name: Mightycause
What it is: A comprehensive online fundraising and donor management platform for nonprofits (Mightycause).
Cost (to organizer): Free ($0/month subscription) plus transaction fees (~1.99% + $0.30) (Mightycause Pricing)
Audience/reach: 76,000+ nonprofits and millions of donors nationwide (Mightycause)
Notes/gotchas: Requires EIN for nonprofit verification; transaction fees can be covered by donors or the organization (Mightycause Support).

Name: PayPal Giving Fund
What it is: A platform that allows 501(c)(3) charities to receive donations from PayPal and its partners like eBay and GoFundMe without transaction fees.
Cost (to organizer): Free (No fee for enrollment or services; 100% of donations made on PayPal platforms are passed to charities) (PayPal).
Self-service level: Manual review/approval (Submission is reviewed within 3-5 business days) (PayPal).
Effort/time: Medium (Requires PayPal Business account, charity status verification, and bank linking) (PayPal).
Audience/reach: Millions of potential donors across PayPal, eBay, GoFundMe, and other partner platforms (PayPal).
Notes/gotchas: California-based charities must complete an additional enrollment form. Payouts are faster for enrolled charities; non-enrolled charities may wait up to 90 days (PayPal).

Name: Qwoted
What it is: A network that connects PR professionals and expert sources (such as nonprofit leaders) with journalists seeking information for stories.
Audience/reach: "Tens of thousands of media professionals" including journalists from the Wall Street Journal, New York Times, and Reuters (Qwoted).
Notes/gotchas: The free Basic plan is limited to 2 pitches per month and includes a 2-hour response delay on media opportunities (Qwoted Pricing). Press release distribution is available through the platform after account creation (Qwoted Help Center).

Name: South Arts
What it is: A Regional Arts Organization (RAO) that provides funding, advocacy, and resources to the arts sector in the Southeastern United States.
Audience/reach: Arts organizations and artists across Alabama, Florida, Georgia, Kentucky, Louisiana, Mississippi, North Carolina, South Carolina, and Tennessee.
Geography: US region (AL, FL, GA, KY, LA, MS, NC, SC, TN)
Notes/gotchas: Requires account creation in Salesforce portal. Requires Unique Entity Identifier (UEI). Specific grants have budget caps (e.g., $500k for Professional Development). Regional restriction to 9 Southeast states. Many grants require a dollar-for-dollar cash match.

Name: The Art Newspaper
What it is: A leading international publication covering news, analysis, and reviews in the art world.
Audience/reach: 50,000+ readership, including collectors, museum directors, and art professionals (The Art Newspaper)
Notes/gotchas: No automated web form for submissions; all press releases and news tips must be pitched via email to the editorial team. Listing in the exhibition section typically follows editorial selection or paid advertising (The Art Newspaper).

Name: Zeffy
What it is: A zero-fee fundraising platform for nonprofits that covers all processing fees.
Audience/reach: Over 100,000 nonprofits (Zeffy)
Notes/gotchas: 100% free for nonprofits; Zeffy covers all transaction and credit card fees by asking donors for an optional tip (Zeffy).

Name: Florida Division of Arts and Culture (formerly Division of Cultural Affairs)
What it is: The state agency responsible for cultural planning and the distribution of state and federal funds for arts and culture in Florida.
Audience/reach: Florida-wide arts and cultural organizations, artists, and public entities. Reach includes thousands of Florida-based nonprofits.
Notes/gotchas: Applications must be submitted through the DOS Grants portal. Organizations must be Florida-based nonprofits or public entities in "active" status on Sunbiz. Requires a UEI number and a Florida Substitute W-9. Deadlines are typically in early July.

Name: Google Ad Grants (Google for Nonprofits)
What it is: A program providing nonprofits with up to $10,000 USD per month in in-kind advertising to appear on Google Search results.
Audience/reach: Billions of Google Search users (global estimate)
Notes/gotchas: Requires 501(c)(3) status and verification via Goodstack/TechSoup. Organizations must maintain a 5% click-through rate (CTR) and follow specific policy requirements to keep the grant. Not available for hospitals, schools, or government entities.

Name: Grants.gov
What it is: The official centralized storefront for all federal grant-making agencies, allowing organizations to find and apply for federal funding opportunities.
Audience/reach: 1,900+ active federal grant opportunities from 26 agencies, reaching all eligible US nonprofit organizations.
Notes/gotchas: Registration is free but requires an active SAM.gov registration (Unique Entity Identifier/UEI), which can take weeks or months to process and requires annual renewal. SAM.gov registration may also require a notarized letter for the entity administrator.

Name: National Endowment for the Arts (NEA)
What it is: Principal grant category (Grants for Arts Projects) providing project-based funding for arts organizations across various disciplines.
Audience/reach: US National (approx. 2,000 awards per year, $65M total program funding) reaching communities across all 50 states, DC, and US jurisdictions.
Notes/gotchas: Requires 1:1 non-federal cost share (cash or in-kind); requires 5-year history of arts programming; requires minimum $20,000 in operating expenses in the most recent fiscal year; requires registration in SAM.gov and Grants.gov (can take weeks). Two-part application process (Grants.gov then NEA Applicant Portal).

Name: Art Market Magazine
What it is: International contemporary fine-art magazine; promotes exhibitions/open calls as paid PR announcements and editorial features.
How to submit a call: Go to 'Submit Your Art News' → fill the form (name, PR announcement, exhibition title/dates, PR text with your external links) → upload images/logo → pay the $80 fee → submit for publication.
Audience/reach: Global audience of artists and collectors reading Art Market Magazine
Notes/gotchas: Treated as a paid PR/editorial announcement (not a submission system). You can include external links to your submission page. Upload up to 15 images + logo. Good for amplification, not a primary listing channel.

Name: Artwork Archive
What it is: Lets organizations POST a standalone open-call LISTING that links out to your own submission URL — no requirement to run submissions on their platform.
How to submit a call: Go to 'New Call for Entry' → choose the standalone Listing ($25 basic / $45 featured) → enter details and your external submission URL (skip their managed-submission option).
Audience/reach: Artists browsing Artwork Archive's Call for Entry discovery feed
Notes/gotchas: TEAM DECISION (Kirsten, Jun 2026): we are going the PAID managed-submission route on Artwork Archive — HOLD on posting a standalone listing for now. (Standalone Listing = $25 basic / $45 featured links to your URL; managed submissions = paid Org plan + $2.50/app.) Don't double-post here while the paid setup is in progress.

Name: ArtConnect
What it is: Professional art network where organizations POST open calls; you can direct artists to your own external submission URL.
How to submit a call: Create an account → 'Post an Opportunity' → enter your call details and put your external submission URL prominently in the description/links ($99/post or $20/mo unlimited).
Audience/reach: Tens of thousands of professional artists worldwide ArtConnect
Notes/gotchas: Verified org-perspective: you can list your own call and point artists to your external submission page in the description/links. Their 'Apply' button defaults to an internal flow, so make your external URL prominent. $99/post or $20/mo unlimited.

Name: ArtShow.com
What it is: Service that promotes juried art shows and competitions to a global artist audience via paid listings.
Cost (to organizer): $45 (Basic, runs until deadline); +$20 image, +$45 featured, +$50 homepage blurb
How to submit a call: Open the juried-shows submission form → pick listing options (Basic $45) → enter contact info + call details, deadline, and your Website/Prospectus links → Continue to Payment → staff reviews and publishes.
Audience/reach: Global artist audience browsing ArtShow.com juried-shows
Notes/gotchas: No account needed. The form has dedicated Website + Prospectus fields so artists go to YOUR site. Listings are NOT instant (staff post manually) and can't be self-edited after submitting.

Name: Call for Entries (C4E)
What it is: Searchable directory of art/photo competitions and juried exhibitions; organizations buy listings to reach the public.
Cost (to organizer): Free tier = members-only (not public); Public: $69/30d or $130/60d (Standard), $169/30d (Featured)
How to submit a call: Go to the Compare/pricing page → pick a tier (Free / Standard / Featured) → 'Order Now' → create or log into an organization account → fill the form with call details, deadline and your external link → pay (for paid tiers) → publishes.
Audience/reach: C4E directory visitors (public listings) / logged-in members (free tier)
Notes/gotchas: Gotcha: the FREE listing is visible only to logged-in C4E members — you need a PAID tier for public visibility. Listings can use an external 'Apply' link to your own submission system.

Name: Hyperallergic
What it is: Online arts publication featuring news, reviews, and a monthly opportunities roundup for artists and creatives.
Audience/reach: 1 million+ unique visitors per month (Hyperallergic)
Notes/gotchas: Does not offer a free self-service listing portal; opportunity features and classifieds are directed to the advertising department via a contact form or Nectar Ads. Editorial tips and announcements can be emailed to tips@hyperallergic.com.

Name: New American Paintings
What it is: Juried showcase/periodical for emerging painters; potential PR/amplification partner, not a submission host.
Audience/reach: Approx. 5,000+ artist reviews annually with a global subscriber base (from site content/context)
Notes/gotchas: New American Paintings is primarily a self-contained juried competition; however, they list a specific contact for advertising inquiries (Liz Morlock) on their Contact page, which is the standard route for an organization looking to reach their audience or propose a collaboration. Direct 'post a call' self-service for external organizations is not found.

Name: NYFA Classifieds / Opportunities
What it is: High-reach NYFA opportunity board where organizations POST open calls; listings link out to your own submission URL.
How to submit a call: Sign up → create a paid classified listing ($95–$315 by duration; nonprofit discount on request) → enter call details + your external submission link.
Audience/reach: 11M+ annual page views, 716K+ arts candidates, and 42K+ newsletter subscribers (NYFA Create Listing)
Best for: open call, exhibition, grant, donor, press
Notes/gotchas: Verified org-perspective: posts link out to YOUR submission page — no lock-in. Small nonprofits (budget ≤ $600k) can request discounted pricing. Strong US/NY reach.

Name: Americans for the Arts
What it is: National job and internship board for the arts sector.
How to submit a call: Create an employer/org account on the ARTSU job bank → post your opportunity → link to your own submission page.
Audience/reach: Arts professionals and organizations nationwide (estimate based on leading national arts advocacy role)
Notes/gotchas: Members get discounted rates ($155 Basic, $239 Premium). Requires account creation to post.

Name: Arts to Hearts Project
What it is: A global media and publishing platform for women artists that curates and publishes open calls and opportunities. (Arts to Hearts Project)
Audience/reach: 160,000+ women creatives worldwide (from homepage) (Arts to Hearts Project)
Best for: open call, exhibition, press (Arts to Hearts Project)
Notes/gotchas: Submissions for curated roundups are selected from a 'pool of submissions' and editorial research; organizers should contact via the contact page for partnerships or proposals (Arts to Hearts Project).

Name: Booooooom Studio
What it is: The partnership and advertising arm of Booooooom, handling inquiries from cultural institutions and brands looking to reach a large creative audience through sponsored content, awards, and ads. (Booooooom Studio)
Audience/reach: 2M+ monthly visitors, global creative audience (Booooooom Studio)
Notes/gotchas: Primary intake for institutions and brands; guaranteed placement or reach typically requires a paid partnership or advertising agreement. Individual artist submissions are handled via a separate member-only portal. (Booooooom Studio)

Name: Create! Magazine
What it is: Independent contemporary art magazine and platform that features artists, open calls, and interviews through print, digital, and social media. (Create! Magazine About)
Audience/reach: 200,000+ global readers and followers (Create! Magazine)
Notes/gotchas: No automated portal for outside organizers to post calls; requires manual partnership inquiry or pitching via email to alicia@createmagazine.com or info@createmagazine.com. (Create! Magazine Contact)

Name: e-flux
What it is: A leading international publishing and distribution platform for contemporary art announcements and press releases.
Audience/reach: 90,000+ art professionals globally (Nearby Café)
Best for: press, exhibition, open call, grant, education
Notes/gotchas: Rates are not publicly listed and require contacting the e-flux team for a quote; submissions are manually reviewed (e-flux About).

Name: eReleases CauseWire
What it is: A specialized press release distribution service for nonprofit organizations, providing access to the PR Newswire network and targeted journalist outreach at discounted rates.
Cost (to organizer): $399 base (Buzz Builder) with 30% first-timer discount; qualify for specific nonprofit rates via form (eReleases).
Audience/reach: 1.7 million+ journalists and influencers, plus national PR Newswire distribution reaching 100+ news sites (eReleases).
Notes/gotchas: Nonprofits must fill out a qualification form to access discounted rates. Every release undergoes manual editorial review. Pricing is per-release with no membership fees (eReleases).

Name: GoFundMe Pro (formerly Classy)
What it is: An enterprise fundraising platform for nonprofits that provides tools for donation forms, peer-to-peer campaigns, event registration, and auctions, leveraging the reach of the GoFundMe donor community.
Audience/reach: ~200 million donors (GoFundMe Pro)
Notes/gotchas: Formerly Classy; now integrated with GoFundMe. Specifically serves US-based 501(c)(3) nonprofits. Pricing is custom and requires contacting their team for a demo and assisted onboarding. Nonprofits can also "claim" their existing GoFundMe page at gofundme.com/charity/claim/search.

Name: GrantStation
What it is: An online grant research platform providing access to searchable databases of private, federal, and international grantmakers, along with strategy tools and educational resources (GrantStation).
Audience/reach: ~20,000+ member organizations (est. based on YouTube), serving nonprofits in the US, Canada, and internationally.
Notes/gotchas: Requires paid membership for full database access. Offers a free weekly newsletter (Insider). Discounts frequently available via TechSoup or seasonal promotions ($119-$150) (Grantsights).

Name: LensCulture
What it is: Global photography platform/magazine; promote your photo open call via their advertising/media options (not a submission host).
Audience/reach: 3M+ monthly reach across platforms, 700,000+ email subscribers, 1.1M Instagram followers (LensCulture Advertising).
Notes/gotchas: Promotional/PR channel for photo audiences. Request media kit for pricing. Not a submission platform — use for amplification only.

Name: Network for Good (Bonterra Nonprofit Hub)
What it is: A donor management and fundraising platform providing CRM tools, online donation pages, and grant-matching resources for nonprofits Bonterra, Network for Good
Cost (to organizer): Starts at ~$200/month (billed annually) + 3%-4.75% transaction fees Zeffy, Merchant Alternatives
Audience/reach: Small-to-mid nonprofits; platform serves over 1,300+ organizations Zeffy
Notes/gotchas: Now part of Bonterra; core fundraising software requires a paid subscription and often a demo, though a free 'Nonprofit Hub' profile is available for basic donor data and grant matching Bonterra, Zeffy

Name: Pinterest Ads
What it is: Visual advertising platform for promoting pins to reach targeted audiences for brand awareness, traffic, and conversions.
Cost (to organizer): $0.00–$0.10 per click, $0.00–$2.00 per conversion, or $0.00–$1.50 per 1,000 impressions. (WebFX, 2026)
Audience/reach: 631 million monthly active users globally, with approximately 106 million in the US and Canada as of Q1 2026. (Pinterest Q1 2026 Results)
Notes/gotchas: Requires a free Pinterest Business account. Nonprofits can select 'Institution/Nonprofit' during business profile setup. (Charity Digital, Pinterest Help Center)

Name: Res Artis
What it is: A worldwide network of arts residencies that provides a platform for organizations to list residency programs and post unlimited open calls for artists.
Cost (to organizer): membership €69 - €700/yr (tiered by organization budget) Res Artis Pricing
How to submit a call: Join/update your member profile, then submit your residency/open-call opportunity for manual review and listing.
Audience/reach: 3,918,039 annual website visitors, 25,000+ newsletter subscribers, and 125,000+ Facebook followers (global artist community) Res Artis Advertising
Notes/gotchas: Res Artis is a vetted network; organizations must submit a detailed application including budget files and images to be approved for membership and listing Res Artis Join Form

Name: This Is Colossal
What it is: An international platform and digital magazine for contemporary art and visual expression (Colossal).
Audience/reach: 1M+ monthly visitors, 200k+ newsletter subscribers, 2.5M+ pageviews (Nectar Ads)
Notes/gotchas: Submissions for the monthly "Colossal Opportunities" roundup should be sent to [email protected]; general editorial tips go to [email protected] High volume means they cannot respond to all inquiries (Colossal Submissions).

Name: Meta Ads Manager
What it is: A comprehensive tool for creating, managing, and tracking advertisements across Meta's platforms (Facebook, Instagram, Messenger).
Audience/reach: Over 3 billion monthly active users globally across Facebook, Instagram, Messenger, and WhatsApp (Meta). For nonprofits, typical CPC is $0.20-$0.80 and CPM is $2-$8 (Vispaico).
Best for: donor, grant, press, open call, exhibition
Notes/gotchas: Requires a Facebook personal account and a Meta Business Suite account. High learning curve. Nonprofit subsidized rates may apply via specialized programs like Meta for Nonprofits (Benly).
